task: STAFF-001 — Staff login and branch-scoped access

scenarios.scenario: Customer-role account calls a staff endpoint hoping client-side nav hiding is the only gate (privilege escalation).
scenarios.rationale: requireStaff runs server-side on every /api/staff/* request and returns 403 for any role not in STAFF_ROLES. Proven by tests E2 (middleware) and E6 (route via supertest). Client-side (staff) route hiding is defense-in-depth, NOT the trust boundary.

scenarios.scenario: Staff A (Branch 1) passes a Branch 2 id to read another branch's data (cross-branch data exfiltration).
scenarios.rationale: The STAFF-001 canary GET /api/staff/me accepts NO branch id from the client — it resolves the caller's own branch via resolveBranchScope(db, userId). assertBranchScope (tested E4/E5) enforces exact-match for future STAFF-002+ routes that do accept a branch id. Branch scope is server-derived from the DB, never client-supplied.

scenarios.scenario: Client attempts to self-assign role=admin or role=staff at signup to gain staff access (role tampering).
scenarios.rationale: better-auth `role` field is input:false (server-owned); any attempt to set it is rejected ('role is not allowed to be set') — proven by the existing auth.integration.test.ts regression which still passes. assigned_branch_id is likewise only set server-side (seed/admin), never via a client-writable path in this ticket.

scenarios.scenario: Unauthenticated request (no session) reaches a staff route (auth bypass).
scenarios.rationale: getSession returns null without a valid session; requireStaff denies with 403. The catch branch also returns 403 (never 500), so malformed/expired session tokens cannot leak a stack trace or fall through to the handler.

scenarios.scenario: Unassigned staff (assignedBranchId null) tries to access branch-scoped data.
scenarios.rationale: assertBranchScope returns false when assignedBranchId is null (tested), so unassigned staff can access nothing on future scoped routes. On the canary, assignedBranch simply returns null — no data leak.

scenarios.scenario: Loading-race: a staff user is briefly shown the customer (tabs) stack or vice-versa during session restore.
scenarios.rationale: isStaffUser and isCustomer both require !isLoading && user!==null; while isLoading is true, neither protected stack mounts and the unauthenticated (auth) guard covers the cold-start beat. No flash of the wrong shell.

scenarios.scenario: Mobile shell fetch fails and silently displays a stale/incorrect branch name.
scenarios.rationale: fetchStaffMe returns null on any error (never throws); useStaffMe surfaces a non-null error and the shell renders 'Branch unavailable'. No silent wrong-branch display. Read-only call on the existing auth session; no new trust boundary introduced.
